page: Microsoft Azure L32s is an LS family virtual machine instance configured with 32 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz, featuring a 40 MB L3 cache. The instance includes 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It is equipped with 5765 GB of local NVMe storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show weak performance. Conversely, multi-threaded execution is significantly stronger; bzip3 multi-threaded decompression ranks in the top 25%, while stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores sit in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth and database operations consistently register average performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L32s offers high local storage density and a substantial memory footprint relative to its processing power. Because single-core performance is limited, this instance is less suited for single-threaded applications or latency-sensitive sequential processing. Instead, it is optimized for workloads that can leverage its massive 5765 GB local storage and parallel processing capabilities, such as large-scale database operations, multi-threaded data decompression, and distributed caching systems like Redis.
description: Microsoft Azure L32s is an LS family virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 5765 GB of local storage. Benchmark data indicates a significant performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks are weak, ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression, perform in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth, database operations, and Redis SET operations consistently achieve average performance. Given its high storage capacity and balanced multi-core capabilities, this instance is suited for storage-heavy workloads, database operations, and parallelized data processing tasks that do not rely on high single-thread clock speeds.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L32s is an LS family instance with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 256 GB RAM, and 5765 GB local storage, offering strong multi-threaded performance alongside weaker single-core speeds.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L32s features 32 dedicated Intel cores, 256 GB RAM, and 5765 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage instance featuring dedicated multi-core processing and substantial memory for parallelized database and data-handling workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 physical cores on x86_64 architecture
256.0 GB of system memory paired with 5765 GB of local storage
Strong multi-threaded performance, highlighted by top-tier bzip3 decompression
Weaker single-core execution speeds and single-threaded processing tradeoffs
Suited for large-scale database operations, Redis, and parallel data workloads
